hero.title: One Key.
hero.sub: The unified gateway to every LLM API. One endpoint, one key, one bill — GPT, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, Qwen and 200+ models, routed in under 40ms.

demo.lead: Chat & vision models are OpenAI-compatible — change one line and switch providers. Image & video run through a single async task API with consistent parameters, returning results via webhook or polling.

lb.title: What the gateway is routing
lb.lead: Most-used models on solayer.org, ranked by tokens processed in the last 24 hours.

show.title: Every modality. One key.
show.lead: Live samples from the models behind the gateway — vision, code, audio, reasoning and Chinese, all through the same endpoint.

c1.a: Revenue dipped 8% in Q3 (the pink marker) after two quarters of growth — likely seasonal, since Q4 recovers to the strongest point on the chart. Recommend annotating the churn spike in September.

c2.q: “Write a rate limiter decorator in Python, token-bucket style.”

c3.a: Transcript ready. Summary: deploy moved to Thursday; two blockers — the staging DB migration and a flaky payment test. Action items assigned to Li (migration) and Sam (test).

c4.q: “A tank fills in 6h via pipe A, 4h via B, drains in 12h via C. All open — when is it full?”
c4.a: Rates: A = 1/6, B = 1/4, C = −1/12 per hour. Net = 2/12 + 3/12 − 1/12 = 4/12 = 1/3. Full tank ÷ net rate = 1 ÷ (1/3) = 3 hours. ✓ verified by dimensional check.

c5.q: “Turn our slogan 'One Key. Every Model.' into a parallel Chinese brand line.”
c5.a: 「一钥通用，万模归一。」— one key unlocks every model; every model converges to one endpoint. Eight characters, balanced couplet, memorable.

flow.title: Three steps. That's it.
flow.lead: Pick a model, send the request, receive results — by webhook push or polling. No SDK migration, no infrastructure.

s1.d: 200+ models behind one endpoint — switch providers by changing a single string.

s2.d: OpenAI-compatible REST. Streaming, tool calls and vision included out of the box.

s3.d: Instant response for chat; webhook push or polling for long-running jobs.

qa.title: Questions, answered.
qa.lead: The things developers ask before their first request.

q1.q: Is solayer.org compatible with the OpenAI SDK?
q1.a: Yes — for chat and vision models the API is fully OpenAI-compatible. Point your existing SDK at our base_url and switch providers by changing the model string. Image and video go through the unified async task API instead.

q2.a: Pay-as-you-go per token (or per generation for media models), aggregated into one monthly bill across all providers. No seats, no minimums. Budgets and alerts are built in.

q3.q: What happens when a provider goes down?
q3.a: Latency-aware routing detects the failure and retries your request on the next-best provider automatically — same model family where possible. You see one consistent endpoint; the failover is invisible.

q4.q: Do you store or train on my prompts?
q4.a: No. Requests are relayed, metered and discarded. Only usage metadata (token counts, latency, model) is retained for analytics and billing. Enterprise plans support zero-log mode.

q5.q: How do I get results from long-running image / video jobs?
q5.a: Two ways: register a webhook and we push the result the moment the task succeeds, or poll GET /v1/tasks/:id at your own pace. Both return the same task object.

ratios.title: Model pricing
ratios.lead: Live billing multipliers for the whole site. Saved changes apply to new requests immediately.

ratios.fetchError: Failed to load pricing options. Refresh the page to retry.
ratios.invalid: Fix the highlighted values before saving.
ratios.confirmMessage: This updates live billing for every user immediately. Wrong values have real money impact.

groups.title: Group ratios
groups.desc: Group ratio multiplies on top of every model's cost price — 1.3 = 30% gross margin.

models.title: Model ratios
models.desc: ModelRatio 1.0 = $2 per million input tokens. Edit either the ratio or the dollar price — they stay in sync.

raw.GroupRatio: User group → multiplier. Applied on top of every model price.
raw.ModelRatio: Model name → ratio. 1.0 = $2 per million input tokens.
raw.CompletionRatio: Model name → output/input price multiplier. Sparse — missing models use the backend default.
raw.invalid: Invalid JSON: must be an object of name → non-negative number.

index.lead: 200+ models behind a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Pick a model, send a request, ship a feature. These docs cover the chat schema, the unified async-task API for image and video, and per-model parameter cheat sheets.

quickstart.title: Three steps to your first response

step1.d: Sign up and copy your sk-key from the console.

step3.d: Change the model string — same SDK, every provider.

tip.title: Try it before you write a line of code.
tip.body: The console playground bills against your own quota and uses the exact relay path your SDK will hit in production. Use it to verify model behavior, copy the equivalent SDK snippet, and ship.

spec.intro: {{model}} from {{family}} routed through the solayer.org gateway. OpenAI-compatible chat completions with streaming, multimodal input, tool calling and adjustable reasoning effort — change the model string in any OpenAI SDK and you're done.

multimodal.d: Text, vision and audio in the same messages array. Pass image_url or input_audio content items inline.

reasoning.d: Tune cost vs. quality with reasoning_effort — low, medium, high, xhigh — without swapping models.

tools.d: Function calling and built-in web search via the tools array. Mutually exclusive with each other.

unified.d: Same /v1/chat/completions surface across every provider. One SDK, one bill, zero migration.

tools.title: Tools & tool_choice
tools.lead: The model can either browse the web or call your functions in a single turn — never both. Pick the behavior that fits your job.

webSearch.title: Web search
webSearch.body: Lets the model fetch live results before answering. Useful for time-sensitive questions, citations and grounding.

fn.title: Function calling
fn.body: Declare functions the model can invoke. Returned as tool_calls with JSON arguments — you execute and feed the result back.

status.200: Success — single object or streamed events
status.400: Bad request — schema or model id
status.429: Rate limited — slow down or upgrade group

next.title: Where to go from here
next.body: Wire authentication and error handling first, then explore the rest of the chat catalog. Image and video models share a different async-task API documented in their respective sections.

quickstart.title: Quickstart
quickstart.lead: Get your first response from solayer.org in under a minute. Same request shape as the OpenAI SDK — only base_url and key change.

s1.p: Replace WAVY_API_KEY with the sk-key you generated in the console. The response shape is identical to OpenAI.

s2.p: Point base_url at our gateway and keep using the SDK you already know. Switch providers by changing the model string.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.lead: Every request needs an Authorization: Bearer sk-key header. Keys are scoped to your account and obey your group quota.

s1.p: Set the Authorization header on every request. Keys are listed and rotated in the console under API Keys.

errors.title: Errors
errors.lead: Errors follow the OpenAI error envelope. Look at error.type and error.code for programmatic handling; error.message is human-readable.

s1.p: All 4xx and 5xx responses return a JSON body with a top-level error object — same shape across providers.

rate.title: Rate limits
rate.lead: Limits are enforced per group and surfaced on response headers so clients can back off without polling a separate endpoint.

s1.p: Inspect x-ratelimit-* headers on every response. The -reset header is a wall-clock duration to full restore.

taskStub.body: {{model}} runs on the unified async-task API at POST /v1/tasks. Send the prompt, receive results via webhook or polling. The full task schema docs are in progress — for now see the home page Demo panel for an example payload.
taskStub.note: Heads up: the spec page for task-based models lands together with the playground image / video tabs.
